content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

December 7, 1995

Bill Maze, Supervisor
County of Tulare
Administration Building
County Civic Center
2800 West Burrel
Visalia, CA 93291

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-95-345

Dear Mr. Maze:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding your duties and responsibilities under the "personal use" and other provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

QUESTION

Is it permissible under the Act for you to use campaign funds to pay to attend and speak at a 1996 Economic Outlook Seminar?

CONCLUSION

Yes. It appears that the use of campaign funds to attend and speak at the 1996 Economic Outlook Seminar bears a reasonable relationship to a governmental purpose under the described circumstances.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000-18995. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-95-345
Page 2


FACTS

     You are a member of the Tulare County Board of Supervisors.
A 1996 Economic Outlook Seminar (a small business conference)
will be held in Visalia on January 16, 1996.  The cost to attend
and speak at the conference is $500.  During our conversation on
November 20, 1995, you indicated that all speakers are required to
pay a $500 speaker's fee.  However, the fee for regular attendees
is $40 per person.

     Based on your additional correspondence and the attached
promotion flyer which we received on November 27, 1995, the
promotion flyer indicates that the sponsors of this event are
Bratcher & Associates, Chicago Title, Jordan Dwinell, Valliwide
Bank, Visalia Press & Printing Center, and the Business Journal.
The flyer also indicates that the $500 fee includes a display
booth, table for eight for the forum and lunch, company name on
your reserved table and a one-quarter page ad in a forecast book.
The $40 fee for a regular attendee includes the forecast forum,
exhibits, forecast booklet, lunch, and a keynote speaker(s).

     You indicated to me in our telephone conversation of
December 5, 1995, that your guests at the luncheon will be the
five county supervisors, the county administrative assistant, the
county chief clerk, and the county administrative officer.

     You further indicated that you will be speaking on matters
which include demographics on county programs relating to
social programs in physical health, mental health, AFDC-GAIN,
welfare eligibility and medical managed care, juvenile delinquency
trends, and the county goals and commitment to build a new
detention facility, county economic development activities, land
use policies, rural valley lands plan, ESA - San Joaquin Valley
biodiversity - County HOP, and the board of supervisors priorities
for 1996.

     Additionally, you state that your presentation will not
include any emphasis of your professional vocation which includes
specialty custom home construction or your farming operation and
that you do not expect to receive any potential monetary benefit
for speaking.

     You also stated that the booth you will receive will display
the county's year-end reports and information about various
programs that the county will be introducing in 1996 and that
neither the name on the reserved table nor the one-quarter page ad
in the forecast book are for personal purposes.

Our File No. A-95-345
Page 3


ANALYSIS

The personal use provisions of the Act apply to the
expenditure of campaign funds (contributions, cash, cash
equivalents, and other assets) held by candidates and elected
officers such as yourself. (Section 82013(a).)

The general rule of the personal use provisions of the Act is
that an expenditure of campaign funds must be, at a minimum,
reasonably related to a political, legislative, or governmental
purpose. (Section 89512; Section 89512.5.)

You indicated that you would be speaking on matters relating
to Tulare County. The focus of the seminar is to address business
trends for the county (finance, real estate, business, and area
demographics) as well as social issues. It is our opinion that
any fees related to your attendance and speaking at the seminar
bears a reasonable relationship to a governmental purpose. In
addition, the booth and ad appear to meet this test.

As for the meals for persons not speaking at the event (the
seven guests you have invited), please note that pursuant to
Government Code Section 89513(f)(1), campaign funds shall not be
used to make personal gifts, unless the gift is directly related
to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. The meals
to the county officials described above would be considered gifts.
We have insufficient facts to determine whether these gifts would
meet the "directly related" standard.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Lynda Doherty
Political Reform Consultant
Legal Division

:ld
